proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `stage_iv` equal to 1 have lower `pfs_months` than patients with `stage_iv` equal to 0 after adjustment for age, ECOG, metastatic sites, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS: pfs_months ~ stage_iv + age_years + ecog_ps + has_brain_mets + liver_mets + bone_mets + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + weight_loss_pct_6mo + crp_mg_l + nlr + hemoglobin_g_dl + tumor_size_cm + ki67_pct + receptor/mutation markers + treatments
analyses.result_summary: `stage_iv` was associated with substantially shorter PFS: adjusted coefficient -1.549 months for stage IV versus non-stage IV (SE 0.011, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each one-point increase in `ecog_ps` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease burden, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.
Patients with `has_brain_mets` equal to 1 have lower `pfs_months` than patients without brain metastases after adjustment for other prognostic factors.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for ecog_ps in the multivariable PFS model.
analyses.result_summary: Higher ECOG performance status was strongly adverse: -1.172 months of PFS per ECOG point (SE 0.0075, p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for has_brain_mets in the multivariable PFS model.
analyses.result_summary: Brain metastases were independently adverse: -0.983 months for `has_brain_mets`=1 versus 0 (SE 0.0168, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `liver_mets` equal to 1 have lower `pfs_months` than patients without liver metastases after adjustment for other clinical burden variables.
Patients with `bone_mets` equal to 1 have lower `pfs_months` than patients without bone metastases after adjustment for other clinical burden variables.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for liver_mets in the multivariable PFS model.
analyses.result_summary: `liver_mets` was not independently associated with PFS after adjustment: -0.018 months (SE 0.0142, p=0.194).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for bone_mets in the multivariable PFS model.
analyses.result_summary: `bone_mets` also was not independently adverse after adjustment: +0.014 months (SE 0.0127, p=0.272).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease burden and treatment.
Higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease burden and treatment.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for albumin_g_dl.
analyses.result_summary: Albumin was favorable: +0.476 months PFS per 1 g/dL higher albumin (SE 0.0101, p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for weight_loss_pct_6mo.
analyses.result_summary: Recent weight loss was adverse: -0.0768 months PFS per 1 percentage point higher six-month weight loss (SE 0.00132, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `ldh_u_l` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment for clinical and biomarker variables.
Higher inflammatory markers `crp_mg_l` and `nlr` are independently associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for ldh_u_l.
analyses.result_summary: LDH had a small but significant adverse association: -0.000518 months per U/L (SE 0.0000630, p=2.22e-16).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficients for crp_mg_l and nlr.
analyses.result_summary: CRP was not independently associated with PFS: -0.000113 months per mg/L (p=0.836). NLR was also not significant: -0.00149 months per unit (p=0.542).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for nlr.
analyses.result_summary: The NLR component of the inflammatory-marker hypothesis was not supported: -0.00149 months per unit (SE 0.00245, p=0.542).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `ki67_pct` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment for stage, ECOG, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.
Larger `tumor_size_cm` is associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment for stage, ECOG, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for ki67_pct.
analyses.result_summary: Ki-67 was adverse: -0.0189 months PFS per 1 percentage point higher `ki67_pct` (SE 0.000419, p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Expanded adjusted OLS coefficient for tumor_size_cm including comorbidities and treatments.
analyses.result_summary: Tumor size was not independently associated with PFS once other variables were included: +0.00136 months per cm (SE 0.00290, p=0.640).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `er_positive` equal to 1 have higher `pfs_months` than patients with `er_positive` equal to 0 after adjustment.
Patients with `pr_positive` equal to 1 have higher `pfs_months` than patients with `pr_positive` equal to 0 after adjustment.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for er_positive.
analyses.result_summary: ER positivity was independently favorable: +0.532 months (SE 0.0149, p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for pr_positive.
analyses.result_summary: PR positivity was not independently favorable after ER and other variables were included: +0.014 months (SE 0.0143, p=0.323).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `her2_positive` equal to 1 have lower `pfs_months` than patients with `her2_positive` equal to 0 after adjustment.
Patients with `her2_low` equal to 1 have higher `pfs_months` than patients with `her2_low` equal to 0 after adjustment.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for her2_positive.
analyses.result_summary: HER2 positivity was independently adverse: -0.471 months (SE 0.0142, p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for her2_low.
analyses.result_summary: HER2-low status carried no independent PFS association: +0.00186 months (SE 0.0113, p=0.869).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `pik3ca_mutation` equal to 1 have lower `pfs_months` than patients without `pik3ca_mutation` after adjustment.
Patients with `brca1_mutation` or `brca2_mutation` equal to 1 have lower `pfs_months` than mutation-negative patients after adjustment.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for pik3ca_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: PIK3CA mutation was strongly adverse: -0.582 months (SE 0.0107, p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficients for brca1_mutation and brca2_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: BRCA1 mutation had a small adverse adjusted association: -0.0685 months (p=0.0337). BRCA2 mutation was not significant: -0.0424 months (p=0.198).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for brca2_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: The BRCA2 component was not supported independently: -0.0424 months (SE 0.0330, p=0.198).

proposed_hypotheses.text: `sex_female`, `diabetes_mellitus`, `hypertension`, `chronic_kidney_disease`, and `heart_failure` are each associated with lower `pfs_months` after adjustment for cancer features and treatments.

analyses.code: Expanded adjusted OLS including sex, comorbidities, clinical variables, biomarkers, and treatments.
analyses.result_summary: `sex_female` was not associated with lower PFS: +0.013 months (p=0.205).

analyses.code: Expanded adjusted OLS coefficient for diabetes_mellitus.
analyses.result_summary: `diabetes_mellitus` was not associated with PFS: +0.0019 months (p=0.878).

analyses.code: Expanded adjusted OLS coefficient for hypertension.
analyses.result_summary: `hypertension` was not associated with PFS: -0.00043 months (p=0.967).

analyses.code: Expanded adjusted OLS coefficient for chronic_kidney_disease.
analyses.result_summary: `chronic_kidney_disease` was not associated with PFS: -0.0124 months (p=0.427).

analyses.code: Expanded adjusted OLS coefficient for heart_failure.
analyses.result_summary: `heart_failure` was not associated with PFS: +0.0071 months (p=0.674).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_tamoxifen` have higher `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_tamoxifen` after adjustment for clinical and biomarker variables.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for treatment_tamoxifen in the multivariable treatment model.
analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen had no adjusted PFS benefit: -0.0076 months (SE 0.0111, p=0.495).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_palbociclib` have higher `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_palbociclib` after adjustment for clinical and biomarker variables.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS coefficient for treatment_palbociclib in the multivariable treatment model without interaction terms.
analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib had a large adjusted main-effect association with longer PFS: +1.102 months (SE 0.0107, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of `treatment_palbociclib` is greater when `er_positive` equals 1 than when `er_positive` equals 0.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS interaction model: pfs_months ~ clinical covariates + treatment_palbociclib + er_positive + treatment_palbociclib:er_positive.
analyses.result_summary: The palbociclib-by-ER interaction was strongly positive: +1.575 additional months in ER-positive patients (SE 0.0232, p<1e-300). Unadjusted palbociclib differences were -0.017 months in ER-negative and +1.577 months in ER-positive patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of `treatment_palbociclib` is greater when `pr_positive` equals 1 than when `pr_positive` equals 0.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS interaction model with treatment_palbociclib:pr_positive.
analyses.result_summary: The palbociclib-by-PR interaction was positive: +1.037 additional months in PR-positive patients (SE 0.0230, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of `treatment_palbociclib` is smaller when `her2_positive` equals 1 than when `her2_positive` equals 0.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS interaction model with treatment_palbociclib:her2_positive.
analyses.result_summary: HER2 positivity markedly attenuated the palbociclib association: interaction -1.346 months (SE 0.0285, p<1e-300). Unadjusted palbociclib differences were +1.350 months in HER2-negative and -0.014 months in HER2-positive patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of `treatment_palbociclib` is smaller when `pik3ca_mutation` equals 1 than when `pik3ca_mutation` equals 0.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS interaction model with treatment_palbociclib:pik3ca_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: PIK3CA mutation strongly attenuated the palbociclib association: interaction -1.677 months (SE 0.0220, p<1e-300). Unadjusted palbociclib differences were +1.659 months in PIK3CA-wildtype and +0.036 months in PIK3CA-mutated patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of `treatment_palbociclib` is greater in the derived subgroup `hr_her2_negative` (ER-positive or PR-positive and HER2-negative) than outside that subgroup.

analyses.code: Derived hr_her2_negative=(er_positive or pr_positive) and not her2_positive; adjusted OLS interaction treatment_palbociclib:hr_her2_negative.
analyses.result_summary: The HR+/HER2-negative interaction was strongly positive: +1.805 months (SE 0.0210, p<1e-300). Within HR+/HER2-negative patients, the adjusted palbociclib coefficient was +1.810 months (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of `treatment_palbociclib` is greatest in the derived `palbo_sensitive_profile` subgroup with `er_positive`=1, `her2_positive`=0, and `pik3ca_mutation`=0.

analyses.code: Derived palbo_sensitive_profile=er_positive and not her2_positive and not pik3ca_mutation; adjusted OLS interaction treatment_palbociclib:palbo_sensitive_profile and subgroup OLS.
analyses.result_summary: The palbociclib-sensitive profile showed the strongest heterogeneity: interaction +2.914 months (SE 0.0170, p<1e-300). In that subgroup, palbociclib's adjusted coefficient was +2.921 months (SE 0.0204, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of `treatment_palbociclib` is smaller in patients with high proliferation, defined as derived `high_ki67_30` equal to 1 when `ki67_pct` is at least 30, than in patients with lower `ki67_pct`.

analyses.code: Derived high_ki67_30=(ki67_pct>=30); adjusted OLS interaction treatment_palbociclib:high_ki67_30.
analyses.result_summary: High Ki-67 substantially attenuated palbociclib benefit: interaction -1.229 months (SE 0.0373, p<1e-300). Unadjusted palbociclib differences were +1.224 months for Ki-67<30 and -0.014 months for Ki-67>=30.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of `treatment_palbociclib` is different in patients with low albumin, defined as derived `low_albumin_lt35` equal to 1 when `albumin_g_dl` is below 3.5, compared with patients with albumin at least 3.5.

analyses.code: Derived low_albumin_lt35=(albumin_g_dl<3.5); adjusted OLS interaction treatment_palbociclib:low_albumin_lt35.
analyses.result_summary: Low albumin did not modify palbociclib benefit: interaction +0.025 months (SE 0.0266, p=0.347). Unadjusted treatment differences were nearly identical in low and normal albumin strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_trastuzumab` have higher `pfs_months`, especially when `her2_positive` equals 1.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS main effect for treatment_trastuzumab plus adjusted interaction treatment_trastuzumab:her2_positive.
analyses.result_summary: Trastuzumab had no adjusted main-effect benefit: -0.0094 months (p=0.460). The HER2-positive interaction was also not significant: +0.0146 months (p=0.696).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS interaction treatment_trastuzumab:her2_positive.
analyses.result_summary: No evidence of greater trastuzumab association in HER2-positive disease: interaction +0.0146 months (SE 0.0375, p=0.696).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_olaparib` have higher `pfs_months` in the derived `brca_any` subgroup, where `brca1_mutation` or `brca2_mutation` equals 1.

analyses.code: Derived brca_any=(brca1_mutation or brca2_mutation); adjusted OLS interaction treatment_olaparib:brca_any and subgroup OLS within brca_any=1.
analyses.result_summary: An unadjusted BRCA-any subgroup difference favored olaparib (+0.346 months), but adjusted evidence did not support it: interaction +0.061 months (p=0.507) and BRCA-any subgroup adjusted treatment coefficient +0.040 months (p=0.635).

analyses.code: Adjusted subgroup OLS among brca_any=1 patients: pfs_months ~ clinical covariates + treatment_olaparib.
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA-any patients, the adjusted olaparib coefficient was only +0.040 months (SE 0.0844, p=0.635).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` have higher `pfs_months` in the derived `triple_negative` subgroup where `er_positive`, `pr_positive`, and `her2_positive` all equal 0.

analyses.code: Derived triple_negative=not er_positive and not pr_positive and not her2_positive; adjusted OLS interaction treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan:triple_negative and subgroup OLS.
analyses.result_summary: Sacituzumab govitecan was not associated with improved PFS in triple-negative disease: interaction -0.0249 months (p=0.599); within triple-negative patients, adjusted treatment coefficient +0.0047 months (p=0.447).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_pembrolizumab` have higher `pfs_months` in the derived `triple_negative` subgroup than outside that subgroup.
The PFS association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` is lower when `autoimmune_disease` equals 1 than when `autoimmune_disease` equals 0.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS interaction treatment_pembrolizumab:triple_negative and subgroup OLS within triple_negative=1.
analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab showed only a non-significant trend toward greater association in triple-negative disease: interaction +0.0665 months (p=0.097); within triple-negative patients, adjusted coefficient +0.0079 months (p=0.133).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS interaction treatment_pembrolizumab:autoimmune_disease.
analyses.result_summary: Autoimmune disease did not significantly modify pembrolizumab association: interaction -0.0085 months (SE 0.0757, p=0.911).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` is higher in patients with `has_brain_mets` equal to 1 than in patients without brain metastases.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS interaction treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan:has_brain_mets after clinical covariate adjustment.
analyses.result_summary: A nominal exploratory interaction suggested a higher sacituzumab govitecan association in patients with brain metastases: +0.160 months (SE 0.0661, p=0.0158). Unadjusted differences were +0.162 months with brain metastases and -0.030 months without brain metastases.
